summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Addition rule for means'?

"Addition rule for means" means: E(X + Y) = E(X) + E(Y) and E(X - Y) = E(X) - E(Y); applies regardless of independence. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Addition rule for variances'?

"Addition rule for variances" means: Var(X + Y) = Var(X) + Var(Y) and Var(X - Y) = Var(X) + Var(Y); ONLY valid when X and Y are independent. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Standard deviation of a sum/difference'?

"Standard deviation of a sum/difference" means: SD(X ± Y) = √(Var(X) + Var(Y)) for independent random variables; note that SDs do NOT simply add. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Linear combination'?

"Linear combination" means: T = aX + bY: E(T) = aE(X) + bE(Y) and Var(T) = a²Var(X) + b²Var(Y) for independent X, Y. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Independence requirement'?

"Independence requirement" means: Variance addition rules require independence; for dependent variables, covariance terms must be included. The other options describe different concepts.
